_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning how noun groups can be expanded with extra words to give a fuller, more detailed description of people, places, things or ideas.

_source.w: We are learning how noun groups can be expanded with extra words to give a fuller, more detailed description of people, places, things or ideas.
y: Expanded noun groups add richness and precision to our writing.
This technique is used by skilled writers to create vivid descriptions without needing extra sentences.
Understanding noun group expansion helps us read and appreciate descriptive writing.
eg: We expand 'the house' to 'the crumbling old stone house on the edge of the cliff'.
We identify expanded noun groups in published texts and discuss what information each part adds.
We practise adding adjectives, classifiers and qualifiers to basic noun groups.
We compare writing with simple noun groups to writing with expanded ones and discuss the difference.
We edit our own writing to expand key noun groups where more detail is needed.
We analyse how an author uses expanded noun groups to create a vivid setting or character description.
